answer:
**Allama Iqbal's *Bang-E-Dara* collections (Parts 1, 2, and 3) contain the most explicit reflections on the nature of *ishq* (divine/passionate love), often as a profound, hidden, transformative force intertwined with beauty (*husn*), anguish (*dard*), and spiritual aspiration—contrasting superficial displays with its sacred essence.[^1][^2][^3]**

These Urdu poems personify *ishq* as a "pearl of purest water" to be concealed from unworthy eyes, urging it to shun public display in a shallow age: "O, Anguish of Love! A pearl of purest water, you; / Be not revealed to the unworthy view."[^4] They explore its ultimate intensity ("the final reach of Your Love"), preferring divine trial over paradise.[^5] One poem directly contemplates *ishq*'s essence relative to *husn*: "From Beauty, Love's own nature is spurred toward perfection."[^6]

**Mirza Ghalib's *Diwan-E-Ghalib* frequently invokes mystical *ishq*-like love experientially (e.g., as a consuming fire defying comfort or reason), but lacks the same overt philosophical dissection of its nature.[^7]**

No works reflect explicitly on *prema* (devotional love) in a philosophical sense; mentions occur in unrelated Dharmaśāstra contexts (e.g., Manusmṛti). Christian Old English poems like *Andreas* use "love" devotionally but narratively, without meta-reflection.[^8] The corpus (37 works, mostly philosophy/classics) covers no other explicit treatments.

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 17 ("Dard-e-Ishq"). [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/17-dard-e-ishq/translation/]
[^2]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 13 ("Husn o Ishq"). [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/13-husn-o-ishq/translation/]
[^3]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 12 ("Tere ishq ki inteha chahta hoon"). [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/12-tere-ishq-ki-inteha-chahta-hoon/translation/]
[^4]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 17, [p-6d19d8](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/17-dard-e-ishq/translation/#p-6d19d8).
[^5]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 12, [p-34168f](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/12-tere-ishq-ki-inteha-chahta-hoon/translation/#p-34168f).
[^6]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 13, [p-7a49e3](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/13-husn-o-ishq/translation/#p-7a49e3).
[^7]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, overview via wiki. [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/wiki/_work.card.md]
[^8]: *Andreas*, ch. 1 (general mentions). [/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/]
